_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Cervical abnormalities / Precancerous conditions / Cervical cancer
clinical_indications: Discrepancy between clinical findings and PAP smear report
Colposcopy showing inadequate delineation of transformation zone/ upper limit of abnormal area not visible

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with history, symptoms, signs, examination findings, indication for procedure and advice for admission
Other investigation report (such as PAP test, HPV test, Colposcopy report, etc.)

claim.label: Detailed indoor case papers clearly indicating the need for performing the procedure
Pictures of gross specimen removed along with size of cone
Histopathology form confirming submission of specimen removed for histopathological examination

ppd_preauth.q: Are detailed Clinical notes all vitals, detailed history, symptoms, signs, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure and advice for admission available?
Was the clinical presentation, physical examination ± supporting investigations indicative of procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Are detailed procedure notes available with indications for the procedure?
Is discharge summary available with follow-up advice at the time of discharge?
Was Histopathology form confirming submission of specimen removed for histopathological examination submitted?
Was there a correlation of biopsy lesion with histopathology report?

common_queries: Clinical presentation, physical examination, or supporting investigations not indicative of the procedure.
Detailed Clinical notes (vitals, history, symptoms, signs, physical examination, indication, admission advice) not available or incomplete.
Other investigation reports (e.g., PAP test, HPV test, Colposcopy report) not provided or incomplete.
Detailed procedure notes are missing or do not contain indications for the procedure.
Discharge summary is missing or does not include follow-up advice.
Histopathology form confirming submission of specimen removed for examination is missing or incomplete.
No correlation found between biopsy lesion and histopathology report.
Pictures of gross specimen removed or its size are missing or incomplete.
Indoor case papers are missing or do not clearly indicate the need for the procedure.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Tertiary level facility (essential criteria) having Well equipped operation theatre with anaesthesia facility.
clinical_key_pointers: Cervical biopsy can be done in several ways (Punch biopsy/ Cone biopsy/ Endocervical curettage (ECC).
Cone biopsy (conization) is a procedure done to remove a sample of abnormal tissue from the cervix.
The tissue thus removed is sent for histopathology examination to test for abnormal/ precancerous conditions/cervical cancer.
It can be done both as a diagnostic and a therapeutic procedure.
contraindications: Too little cervix to excise due to previous excision
Pregnancy (relative contraindication), should only be performed incases of strong suspicion of invasive cancer
